generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) are correct and resolve all references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Output assignments should use the `assign` keyword in implementation annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on expression and type correctness.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to repair syntax errors by modifying component connections or subcomponents.
